allow:
Bash(gh release create v1.1.0 "TidyPackRat-v1.1.0-Setup.msi" "TidyPackRat-v1.1.0-Portable.zip" --title "TidyPackRat v1.1.0" --notes "$(cat <<''EOF''
## TidyPackRat v1.1.0

### New Features
- **File Size Threshold UI** - New control to skip files smaller than a specified size (KB)
- **Configuration Backup** - Automatic backup of settings before each save
- **Input Validation** - Validates time format, thresholds, and folder paths before saving

### Improvements
- Improved time validation with bounds checking (HH:mm format, 0-23 hours, 0-59 minutes)
- Better worker script discovery with expanded search paths
- Enhanced error logging in Task Scheduler operations
- More secure temp file handling using GUIDs

### Security
- Path validation blocks system-critical folders (Windows, Program Files, root drive)
- Replaced Invoke-Expression with direct command execution in build script
- Improved WiX Toolset detection (searches PATH and common install locations)

### Documentation
- Added Input Validation and Configuration Backup sections
- Added Security Considerations to troubleshooting guide

### Downloads
- **TidyPackRat-v1.1.0-Setup.msi** - Windows installer (recommended)
- **TidyPackRat-v1.1.0-Portable.zip** - No installation required, run from any folder

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ProfessorMoose74/TidyPackRat/compare/v1.0.1...v1.1.0
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
v1.2.0: Major feature update for Microsoft Store readiness

New Features:
- System tray integration with minimize-to-tray and tray menu
- Dark/Light theme toggle with full UI theming
- Statistics dashboard tracking files organized and space saved
- Real-time file watching with automatic organization
- Undo/Rollback functionality for recent file moves
- Custom category creation via GUI dialog
- Windows toast notifications for file organization events
- Sound effects (optional) for audio feedback
- Export/Import settings functionality
- High DPI and Per-Monitor DPI v2 support
- Accessibility: AutomationProperties and keyboard shortcuts (Alt+T/R/V/S)

New Files:
- PRIVACY.md - Privacy policy for Microsoft Store
- STORE_LISTING.md - Microsoft Store submission content
- docs/msix-packaging.md - MSIX packaging guide
- Models: Statistics.cs, UserPreferences.cs, MoveHistory.cs
- Helpers: PreferencesManager.cs, NotificationHelper.cs, ThemeManager.cs
- Services: FileWatcherService.cs
- Themes: LightTheme.xaml, DarkTheme.xaml
- AddCategoryDialog.xaml/cs - Custom category dialog

UI Overhaul:
- Tabbed interface: Dashboard, Organization, Schedule, Settings
- Modern card-based statistics display
- Recent activity feed on dashboard

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Rename TidyPackRat to TidyFlow for Flow family branding

Application Rename:
- Renamed all namespaces from TidyPackRat to TidyFlow
- Renamed project file from TidyPackRat.csproj to TidyFlow.csproj
- Updated all XAML files with TidyFlow class names
- Renamed worker script to TidyFlow-Worker.ps1
- Updated all UI text references to TidyFlow
- Changed task scheduler task name to TidyFlow-AutoOrganize
- Updated config paths to use TidyFlow folder names
- Updated export file extension from .tprconfig to .tfconfig

Documentation Updates:
- Updated README.md with TidyFlow branding
- Updated PRIVACY.md with TidyFlow name
- Updated STORE_LISTING.md with TidyFlow name and v1.2.0 features
- Updated default-config.json with TidyFlow app name

Note: Git repository name remains ''tidy-pack-rat'' but all in-app
references now use the TidyFlow name for the Flow product family.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\rober\\Git\\tidy-pack-rat" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
v1.2.5: Fix PowerShell crash on Windows 11 Insider builds \(26200.x\)

Critical Fixes:
- Replace $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey\(\) with robust Wait-ForKeyPress function
  using fallback chain: cmd /c pause → Read-Host → RawUI.ReadKey → Sleep
- Add null check for Process.Start\(\) return in TaskSchedulerHelper
- Add maxAttempts limit \(10000\) to prevent infinite loops in duplicate
  filename generation \(both C# and PowerShell\)
- Fix FileSystemWatcher race condition with proper event unsubscription
- Add IOException/UnauthorizedAccessException handling for File.Move
- Fix unsafe registry value cast in ThemeManager

These changes resolve Microsoft Store rejection due to PowerShell worker
crashing during "Preview Changes" on Windows 11 build 26200.6901.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\rober\\Git\\tidy-pack-rat" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
v1.2.6: Fix PowerShell worker crash on Windows 11 24H2 \(build 26100.7171\)

Root Cause:
Windows 11 24H2 introduced CMD regressions that cause `cmd /c pause` to
crash in certain contexts including MSIX packages. The PowerShell worker
was using this as the "most reliable" pause method, but it now crashes
the entire PowerShell window on Windows 11 24H2.

Fixes:
- Remove cmd /c pause entirely - CMD has known issues on Windows 11 24H2
- Remove $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey\(\) - can crash certain console hosts
- Simplify Wait-ForKeyPress to use only Read-Host \(pure PowerShell\)
- Add try/catch fallbacks around all Write-Host -ForegroundColor calls
  in case color output fails on constrained console hosts
- Update MaxVersionTested to 10.0.26200.0 for Windows 11 Insider support

This resolves Microsoft Store rejection where "Preview Changes" caused
the PowerShell window to crash immediately on Windows 11 build 26100.7171.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\rober\\Git\\tidy-pack-rat" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
v1.2.7: Fix PowerShell crash on Windows 11 Insider build 26200.x

Root Cause:
Windows 11 Insider builds \(26200.6901\) have PowerShell compatibility
issues with Set-StrictMode and colored console output that cause
immediate crashes when launching the worker script.

Fixes:
- Remove Set-StrictMode -Version Latest entirely - "Latest" introduces
  breaking strict checks on newer PowerShell versions in Insider builds
- Remove ALL -ForegroundColor arguments from Write-Host calls - colored
  output crashes the console host on build 26200.x
- Add immediate "TidyFlow Worker starting..." output before any
  initialization to confirm script launch
- Change ErrorActionPreference from "Stop" to "Continue" to prevent
  immediate termination on minor issues
- Simplify Wait-ForKeyPress and Write-Log functions \(no color output\)

Note: These are cosmetic/compatibility changes only. The core file
organization logic remains unchanged and was never broken.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
